response.description_long:
Prior authorization is the top administrative burden in specialty care. Staff spend hours on payer portals and phone calls; patients wait days for approvals; denials arrive for missing documentation that was sitting in the chart all along. Clinicore automates this process for specialty clinics.

The platform reads the order from the EHR, assembles the payer-specific documentation, submits it electronically, and tracks every request to a decision. It integrates with Epic, Cerner, and athenahealth. When a clinician places an order that needs authorization, Clinicore detects it, pulls the clinical notes and labs that the payer's policy requires, and assembles the submission. A rules engine encodes payer policies and keeps them current; a clinical operations team reviews edge cases before anything goes out. Status updates flow back into the EHR, and staff see one queue instead of a dozen portals.

Clinicore serves specialty practices and clinic groups in oncology, cardiology, rheumatology, and imaging — from single-site clinics to groups with 40 locations. Clinics using Clinicore report authorizations submitted in minutes instead of hours, first-pass approval rates above 90 percent, and staff time on authorizations cut by more than half.

The company charges per provider per month, with volume pricing for groups. Founded in 2020 in Nashville, TN by a practice administrator and two health-data engineers, Clinicore now has 60 people and is remote-friendly.
